detailed_context: Alfredo was trying to commit a fix for Windows compatibility issues and receive step-by-step instructions for using the app. Orion successfully created a commit addressing the Unix-only 'which' command by changing it to 'where' for Windows, but then encountered a critical error when trying to spawn a process. The ENOENT error indicates the system couldn't find the Claude executable at the specified paths. This failure prevented Orion from completing the step-by-step instructions Alfredo requested. The root cause appears to be path resolution issues, likely stemming from the recent Windows compatibility fix not fully addressing all path-related problems. Alfredo is likely frustrated because the core task (committing the fix and providing instructions) was interrupted by a system-level error that undermines the very fix that was just implemented. Orion should have either gracefully handled the path resolution error or provided alternative methods to complete the instructions without relying on the problematic spawn mechanism.
